mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 350775M as of June 2005 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative in > 40% of rolling 6-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, the Fed is in a restrictive monetary stance that increases discount rates and borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.04% as of June 2005, approaching restrictive threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, or average forward return in triggered regime is not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment (PAYEMS) combined with stable or declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals tight labor markets and wage pressure, which constrains corporate profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133537k and UNRATE at 5.0% as of June 2005 indicate tight labor market
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in rolling 12-month regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines signal weakening economic activity and reduced corporate output, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO declines > 0.2%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal broad-based demand strength. When both rise simultaneously, equity valuations expand due to improved earnings visibility and economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2009k and RSAFS at 350775M as of June 2005 show robust demand
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.20) in rolling 12-month regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the Fed maintains FEDFUNDS below 3.5%, creating a negative real rate environment that initially supports equities but signals future rate hikes, producing negative returns over 60 trading days as the market anticipates policy tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 194.1 and FEDFUNDS at 3.04% as of June 2005
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect between inflation and policy rate is not significant (p > 0.15) in rolling 12-month regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.2% in a single month, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% as of June 2005, near trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE > 5.2%, or average forward return in triggered regime is not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly decelerates below 2.5% annualized rate, signaling weakening economic momentum and reduced corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices terminal growth expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 2.5%, or Rank IC between GDP growth and forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
